evals.prompt: Should I use `containerEnv` or `remoteEnv` for my `DATABASE_URL` in devcontainer.json? The URL doesn't change, it's just different per developer.
evals.expected_output: Recommends containerEnv for a static value, explains the difference (all processes see containerEnv vs remoteEnv is client-specific/avoids rebuild), cites the Dev Container metadata reference.

assertions.text: recommends-containerenv: Response recommends containerEnv for a static DATABASE_URL
explains-all-processes: Explains that containerEnv is visible to all processes in the container (not just the VS Code client)
explains-remoteenv-use-case: Explains that remoteEnv is client-specific and/or avoids rebuilds for dynamic values
cites-reference: Cites the Dev Container metadata reference or containers.dev

evals.prompt: I use Podman (rootless) on my Linux laptop and added `--userns=keep-id` and `--security-opt=label=disable` to `runArgs` in devcontainer.json to fix a file permission issue. But now my teammates on Docker Desktop (Mac and Windows) get errors when they try to start the container. How do I make devcontainer.json work for both runtimes?
evals.expected_output: Explains that runArgs is applied unconditionally regardless of runtime. Recommends the shim script workaround (a script at dev.containers.dockerPath that adds Podman-specific args only when the command is 'podman run'). Does NOT suggest maintaining two separate devcontainer.json files as the primary solution. Cites the devcontainers community discussion or tools reference.

assertions.text: explains-runargs-unconditional: Explains that runArgs is applied regardless of whether the runtime is Docker or Podman (no conditional support in devcontainer.json itself)
recommends-shim-script: Recommends a shim/wrapper script set via dev.containers.dockerPath (or equivalent) that conditionally adds Podman-specific args
does-not-recommend-two-configs-as-primary: Does not present maintaining two separate devcontainer.json files as the recommended solution
cites-source: Cites a source (devcontainers discussion, tools reference, or official docs)

evals.prompt: I want to write a custom dev container Feature that installs our internal CLI tool from a private GitHub release. What are the key things I need to get right in `devcontainer-feature.json` and `install.sh`?
evals.expected_output: Covers required devcontainer-feature.json fields (id, version), install.sh OS detection, _REMOTE_USER for non-root, idempotency, handling private GitHub releases (auth/token), redundant install paths. Points to feature-starter and authoring guide.

assertions.text: devcontainer-feature-json-fields: Covers required devcontainer-feature.json fields (id and version at minimum)
os-detection-in-install-sh: Mentions OS/distro detection in install.sh and exiting with a clear error if the OS is unsupported
remote-user-env-var: Mentions _REMOTE_USER or _REMOTE_USER_HOME for handling non-root container users
idempotency: Mentions designing the Feature to be idempotent (safe to install multiple times with different options)
private-release-auth: Addresses the private GitHub release aspect (e.g. token/auth strategy via options or environment variables)
points-to-authoring-guide: References the feature-starter repo or the containers.dev Feature authoring guide
